content.full_text:
November 1, 1978


Ms. Frances Prescott
Riverside County Democratic
Central Committee
6618 Duke Street
Riverside, California 92506

Dear Ms. Prescott:

There is nothing in the Political Reform Act that requires a
democratic club or any other entity which acts as an agent of
a registered committee to secure their own identification number
from the Secretary of State.

The Central Committee is obligated to report all the activity of
your club on their campaign statements. If the club receives
contributions which it subsequently turns over to the Central
Committee it may be an intermediary and if so must be listed as
such on the Central Committee campaign statement.

If your club does begin to act as an independent entity, however,
(e.g. depositing contributions in a bank account other than that
of the Central Committee's for the payment of expenses, etc.) it
will be required to register as a committee with the Secretary of
State and file campaign statements.

If you wish additional assistance, please do not hesitate to call
either our Los Angeles or Sacramento office.

Very truly yours,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

State of California                                                                                          Franchise Tax Board


M e m o r a n d u m

To     :  Michael Bennett, Executive Director              Date   :  September 14, 1978
          Fair Political Practices Commission
          1100 K Street                                     File No.:
          Sacramento, CA 95814
                                                           Telephone: ATSS (        )
                                                                      (        )


From  :  Norman L. Fuller


Subject:  Inquiry from Riverside County Democratic Central Committee


          When responding to our questionnaire, the subject committee asked a question
          which properly should be asked of the Commission. The question is as
          follows:

               Can a democratic club, chartered by the Riverside County
               Democratic Central Committee, use the central committee's
               Secretary of State I.D. number if they collect contributions
               in the name of the central committee and the club name, or
               does the club have to secure their own Secretary of State
               I.D. number?

          Please have your staff prepare a response to be sent to:

               Riverside County Democratic Central Committee
               c/o Frances K. Prescott
               6618 Duke Street
               Riverside, CA 92506


                    Norman L. Fuller
          Acting Chief, Political Reform Audit Division

          cc: Frances K. Prescott

sections.question: Can a democratic club, chartered by the Riverside County Democratic Central Committee, use the central committee's Secretary of State I.D. number if they collect contributions in the name of the central committee and the club name, or does the club have to secure their own Secretary of State I.D. number?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a democratic club chartered by a county central committee may use the central committee's Secretary of State identification number when collecting contributions in both names, or must obtain its own identification number.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/referral from the Franchise Tax Board, not an FPPC advice letter response. The document contains the question but no FPPC conclusion or advice. Text is heavily corrupted/garbled in opening paragraph, appears to be an OCR error on original document. Core question and routing information extracted successfully.

embedding.summary: This is a memorandum from the California Franchise Tax Board to the FPPC Executive Director forwarding a legal question from the Riverside County Democratic Central Committee regarding whether a subordinate democratic club can share the central committee's Secretary of State I.D. number when collecting contributions.
